raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The section advances two distinct contribution claims — SG-Constraint as a benchmark artifact and tube-MPC dominance as a methodological finding; reviewing requires assessing whether each claim is coherent, properly scoped, and mutually consistent with the other.","R1.2":"The inferential chain (shared PRNG disturbance traces → fair comparison → tube-MPC dominance → prior benchmarks inadequate) contains potential logical gaps, e.g., whether identical noise realizations are sufficient to neutralize all confounds between model-based and learning-based controllers.","R1.3":"All load-bearing numbers (0.81 ± 0.03, 0.62 ± 0.08, 0.47 ± 0.11, 'one to two orders of magnitude' on tasks 7–10, the (1 − violation_rate)² rubric weight) are specific enough that misalignment with the actual experimental outputs would directly invalidate the comparison narrative.","R1.4":"The assertion that tube-MPC 'dominates' and that this margin has been concealed by 'prior ad-hoc benchmarks' is a strong novelty claim; assessing it requires placing the gap against the existing safe-RL and robust-control evaluation literature, not just internal numbers.","R1.7":"Park and Wei (2024) is the sole cited construction for learned disturbance sets and is architecturally load-bearing in the methodology claim; correctness of attribution, year, and adequacy of related-work coverage around disturbance-set learning must be verified."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the review prioritize technical soundness — evidence-claim alignment, logical consistency, and contribution validity — or writing quality and prose clarity?"}

rationale.R1.1: The section advances two distinct contribution claims — SG-Constraint as a benchmark artifact and tube-MPC dominance as a methodological finding; reviewing requires assessing whether each claim is coherent, properly scoped, and mutually consistent with the other.
rationale.R1.2: The inferential chain (shared PRNG disturbance traces → fair comparison → tube-MPC dominance → prior benchmarks inadequate) contains potential logical gaps, e.g., whether identical noise realizations are sufficient to neutralize all confounds between model-based and learning-based controllers.
rationale.R1.3: All load-bearing numbers (0.81 ± 0.03, 0.62 ± 0.08, 0.47 ± 0.11, 'one to two orders of magnitude' on tasks 7–10, the (1 − violation_rate)² rubric weight) are specific enough that misalignment with the actual experimental outputs would directly invalidate the comparison narrative.
rationale.R1.4: The assertion that tube-MPC 'dominates' and that this margin has been concealed by 'prior ad-hoc benchmarks' is a strong novelty claim; assessing it requires placing the gap against the existing safe-RL and robust-control evaluation literature, not just internal numbers.
rationale.R1.7: Park and Wei (2024) is the sole cited construction for learned disturbance sets and is architecturally load-bearing in the methodology claim; correctness of attribution, year, and adequacy of related-work coverage around disturbance-set learning must be verified.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review prioritize technical soundness — evidence-claim alignment, logical consistency, and contribution validity — or writing quality and prose clarity?
